# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create an Istio PeerAuthentication policy for our microservices namespace. Configure mTLS mode to allow both plaintext and encrypted traffic for backward compatibility. This is for our Kubernetes cluster during migration to service mesh.
# Category: istio_permissive_mtls

apiVersion: security.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: PeerAuthentication
metadata:
  name: microservices-peer-auth
  namespace: microservices
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: microservices-peer-auth
    app.kubernetes.io/part-of: service-mesh
    app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: istio
  annotations:
    description: "PeerAuthentication policy for microservices namespace during migration to service mesh. Allows both plaintext and mTLS traffic for backward compatibility."
spec:
  # PERMISSIVE mode allows both plaintext and mutual TLS traffic
  # This is recommended during migration to ensure services that
  # have not yet been onboarded to the mesh can still communicate
  mtls:
    mode: PERMISSIVE
  # Optional: Override mTLS mode for specific ports if needed
  # For example, health check ports may need to remain plaintext
  portLevelMtls:
    # Health check port - keep permissive for probes from kubelet
    8080:
      mode: PERMISSIVE
    # Metrics port - allow Prometheus scraping without mTLS
    9090:
      mode: DISABLE
  selector:
    matchLabels: {}
